questions.text: The area under the curve y = f(x) from x = a to x = b is given by:
questions.explanation: The area bounded by the curve y = f(x), x-axis, and lines x = a, x = b is given by definite integral ∫[a to b] f(x)dx

questions.text: The area of the region bounded by y = x², x = 0, x = 2 is 8/3 square units.
questions.explanation: Area = ∫[0 to 2] x² dx = [x³/3][0 to 2] = 8/3 - 0 = 8/3 sq units

questions.text: The area enclosed by a circle x² + y² = a² is ________.
questions.explanation: Area of circle with radius a is πa². This can be found using integration: 4 × ∫[0 to a] √(a²-x²)dx

questions.text: How do you find the area between two curves?
questions.correctAnswer: Area between curves y = f(x) and y = g(x) from x = a to x = b is ∫[a to b] |f(x) - g(x)| dx
questions.explanation: The area between two curves is the integral of the absolute difference of the functions over the given interval.
